text.title: Status Report Examples
text.summary: Worked examples showing the four audience-tailored emissions from one source spreadsheet.

example.title: Week 7 of 12 — four emissions from one sheet
example.note: Source: one Notion DB with workstreams (Postgres migration AMBER, Auth/SSO GREEN, API rate-limit GREEN, Docs RED). CEO emission (240 chars, 5 lines): "On plan. 58% complete · 51% of budget · 5 weeks to UAT. Risk: vendor SSO library on hold pending security review." PMO emission (1200 chars, 30 lines): RAG table first (Postgres AMBER, Auth GREEN, API GREEN, Docs RED), one-sentence prose per Amber/Red row. Sponsor emission (1600 chars, 35 lines): Scope changes, 3 blockers (vendor SSO, replica lag, docs translator capacity), 2 decisions needed. Leadership emission (2100 chars, 40 lines): Business-impact narrative ("Auth migration completed; users now SSO-enabled with no support tickets"); no component names; PM confidence statement. single_source_verified=true. Validator: PASS.

example.title: One-size-fits-all that the methodology replaces
example.note: Single 1400-word "weekly status" emailed to alice@client (CEO), bob@client (PMO), carol@client (Sponsor), dave@internal (VP). Week 4: open rate 28%. CEO scrolled past line 2. PMO can't find RAG in the prose. Sponsor's blocker detail buried at the bottom. VP gets raw Postgres terminology and forwards "I don't know what this means". Validator BLOCKS: f1 (CEO line_count > 5), f2 (Leadership contains_raw_technical), f4 (audience-template mismatched), single_source_verified ambiguous. Repair: split into 4 templates, render from one source.
